name: Ethan
communication_level: verbal but heavily echolalic; uses delayed echolalia and scripted phrases from Weather Channel and YouTube tornado videos; emerging spontaneous 2-3 word utterances when highly motivated
interests: ceiling fans (knows brands and blade counts)
subway and transit maps (memorizes entire systems)

seeks: visual spinning objects (fans, wheels, washing machines)
proprioceptive input (crashing into bean bags, heavy lifting)
avoids: wet or sticky textures (glue, paint, wet sand)
clothing tags and seams (only wears tagless shirts inside-out)
calming_strategies: heavy work tasks (carrying books to library, pushing cart)

reinforcers: 3 minutes watching ceiling fan videos on iPad

iep_goals.description: Ethan will use spontaneous (non-echolalic) speech to make requests, comment, or respond to questions in 7 out of 10 opportunities across classroom settings (circle time, centers, lunch), as measured by speech-language pathologist and teacher observation tally.
iep_goals.measurement_method: SLP and teacher observation tally  -  spontaneous vs. scripted utterances

trial_history.notes: Mostly scripted responses from Weather Channel. Said 'I want fan' spontaneously at center time  -  first clear request this month.
Used 'more crackers' at snack unprompted. Still echoing peer responses during circle time but tone is more intentional.
Breakthrough during weather unit  -  spontaneously told aide 'tornado watch today' while looking at window. Motivated topic pulls out real language.
Holding steady. Spontaneous speech still mostly around weather and fans. Trying to generalize to other topics with visual supports.
Said 'no thank you' to peer offering Play-Doh (avoids wet textures but used polite refusal!). SLP thrilled with pragmatic function.
Strong session. Used 'look  -  seven birds!' on playground spontaneously. Asked aide 'what for lunch?' without modeling. Echolalia still present but ratio shifting.

iep_goals.description: Ethan will form uppercase and lowercase letters legibly (recognizable to an unfamiliar reader) on lined paper, completing 8 out of 10 letters correctly in a writing sample, as measured by bi-weekly OT writing probes.
iep_goals.measurement_method: OT bi-weekly writing probe  -  legibility scoring rubric

trial_history.notes: Grip pressure too heavy  -  breaking pencil tips. OT introduced pencil grip and slant board. Letters E, T, H recognizable but inconsistent sizing.
Slant board helping. Can write name legibly most days. Lowercase letters still very large  -  not staying on lines.
Plateau. Gets frustrated and starts scripting 'the weather today is...' when writing gets hard. Avoids glue stick activities entirely.
OT tried weather-themed tracing worksheets (tornado paths, wind patterns). Slightly more engaged but letter formation still inconsistent.
Still at 45%. Uppercase letters stronger than lowercase. Resists writing tasks longer than 5 minutes. May need to explore adaptive tools.
Third session at 45%  -  clearly plateaued. OT recommending we try a vibrating pen and shorter writing bursts with fan breaks between.

iep_goals.description: Ethan will engage in interactive play with a peer (turn-taking, sharing materials, or cooperative activity) for at least 5 minutes during 5 out of 10 free play opportunities, as measured by teacher/aide observation log.
iep_goals.measurement_method: teacher/aide observation log during free play and recess

trial_history.notes: Mostly parallel play. Sits near peers at block center but builds alone. Will tolerate a peer nearby if they don't touch his structures.
Paired with Caleb who also likes maps. They looked at a subway map together for 4 minutes  -  closest to interactive play yet.
Caleb absent this week  -  Ethan went back to solo play. Interest-based pairing seems critical.
Caleb back. Built a 'subway station' together with blocks. Ethan directed but did hand Caleb pieces  -  emerging cooperation.
Played weather reporter game with Caleb and aide. Ethan was the forecaster, Caleb moved cloud cutouts. Lasted 7 minutes with support.
Best session yet  -  initiated play with Caleb at recess ('want to find tornadoes?'). Played for 6 minutes before needing a spin break. Slow but genuine progress.
